name: create
context: Create a single Claude message completion with full parameter control via POST /v1/messages
info: The create sub-skill provides a focused reference for the synchronous (non-streaming) Messages API call. This is the most common operation: send a request, wait for the complete response. The endpoint is POST /v1/messages. You must provide model, max_tokens, and messages. The response arrives as a single JSON object with the complete content, stop_reason, and usage. For streaming, see [[anthropic/messages/stream]]. For batch processing, see [[anthropic/batch]]. The SDK methods are `client.messages.create()` in Python and `client.messages.create()` in TypeScript.
triggers: send one message and get response

sections.title: Basic usage
sections.body: Call `client.messages.create()` with `model`, `max_tokens`, and `messages`. The response is a `Message` object with `content` (array of blocks), `stop_reason`, and `usage`. Access text via `response.content[0].text`. This is synchronous — the call blocks until the full response is ready.

sections.title: Vision and image input
sections.body: Pass images as content blocks with type 'image'. Source can be base64 (with media_type and data) or URL. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP. Images are processed as input tokens. Combine image and text blocks in the same message for visual Q&A.

sections.title: Error handling
sections.body: The SDK raises typed exceptions: `AuthenticationError` (401), `RateLimitError` (429), `BadRequestError` (400), `APIError` (generic). For 429s, read the `retry-after` header and back off. For 500s, retry with exponential backoff. The SDK includes automatic retries for transient errors.

links.context: Parent skill with full parameter reference
Add tool definitions to the create call

related.note: Implements the synchronous create path of the Messages API
Use streaming for real-time token-by-token output
Use batch for high-volume async processing at 50% discount

scripts.name: Create Message
scripts.inline:
import anthropic
import json
import os
import sys

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

model = os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_MODEL", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
max_tokens = int(os.environ.get("MAX_TOKENS", "1024"))
user_input = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "Hello, Claude"
system_prompt = os.environ.get("SYSTEM_PROMPT", "")

kwargs = {
    "model": model,
    "max_tokens": max_tokens,
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user_input}]
}
if system_prompt:
    kwargs["system"] = system_prompt

response = client.messages.create(**kwargs)

print(response.content[0].text)
print(json.dumps({"stop_reason": response.stop_reason, "input_tokens": response.usage.input_tokens, "output_tokens": response.usage.output_tokens}, indent=2))

author: dojo-community
